pattern: A greedy selection that takes the highest values first while a per-label counter enforces the usage cap, teaching constrained top-k picking.
hints: You always want the biggest values, but each label can only contribute a limited number of items.
Sort items by value descending and walk them, skipping any whose label has already hit its limit.
Maintain a count per label; add a value only if its label count is below useLimit and you have taken fewer than numWanted items overall, then stop once you reach numWanted.

edge_inputs.description: first example, one item per label allowed
second example, two of the same label allowed
third example skips a capped label
useLimit forces skipping a higher value
zero-valued items still count toward limits but add nothing

stress.description: many items across a small label space to exercise the per-label cap
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    values = [rng.randrange(0, 20001) for _ in range(n)]
    labels = [rng.randrange(0, 20) for _ in range(n)]
    numWanted = rng.randrange(1, n + 1)
    useLimit = rng.randrange(1, n + 1)
    return [values, labels, numWanted, useLimit]
stress.note: narrow label range makes the limit frequently bind
